chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper explicitly states: "The code and relevant result reproduction scripts are available at https://github.com/imperial-qore/CAROL." This provides a working link to the public repository.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation uses a physical testbed consisting of 16 Raspberry Pi 4B nodes, which constitutes real hardware deployment.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: A formal 'Fault Model' section exists (Section III.A), explicitly stating they consider a byzantine failure model and detailing the types of faults considered (CPU overload, RAM contention, Disk attack, DDOS attack).
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides rigorous, data-driven analysis of trade-offs, quantifying reductions in energy consumption (16.5%), deadline violation rates (17.0%), and resilience overheads (35.6%) compared to baselines.

major_weakness: The paper is exceptionally strong across all criteria; the only minor area for potential critique is that the complexity of the model (GON) might obscure some aspects of its practical implementation details beyond what is provided.
